recap: Germany's 24-year unbeaten penalty shootout record in World Cups came to an end when Paraguay's José Canale converted the decisive spot-kick after a 1-1 draw in Boston, with Jonathan Tah's crucial miss handing the South Americans a 4-3 victory in sudden death. Julio Enciso's unmarked header gave Paraguay an unlikely lead against the run of play, but Kai Havertz equalized after the interval before a VAR-disallowed Tah header in extra-time shifted momentum decisively toward the visitors. Germany's dominance—including 79 percent possession in the first half—proved futile against Paraguay's disciplined defensive wall, forcing the tie to the lottery that ultimately ended their tournament hopes.
